ucs.title: [LLM] Malteiro Mispadu delivery chain: HTML smuggling -> MSI -> VBS/AutoIt loader
ucs.description: Hunts the Mispadu banking trojan delivery chain Malteiro has used since 2019: spearphishing HTML attachment that drops/links a ZIP or MSI, msiexec spawning wscript/cscript executing Mispadu's VBS dropper, often followed by an AutoIt-based loader. This sequence (HTML -> msiexec -> VBS) chained with Spanish/Portuguese-language lures is highly characteristic of Malteiro / Mispadu MaaS operations targeting LATAM (esp. Mexico) and Iberia.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=msiexec.exe (Processes.process_name IN (wscript.exe, cscript.exe, powershell.exe, cmd.exe, AutoIt3.exe, AutoIt3_x64.exe)) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_guid _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name=*.html OR Filesystem.file_name=*.htm OR Filesystem.file_name=*.zip OR Filesystem.file_name=*.msi) (Filesystem.file_path=*\Downloads\* OR Filesystem.file_path=*\AppData\Local\Temp\* OR Filesystem.file_path=*Outlook*) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | stats values(file_name) as drop_files by dest] | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email where (All_Email.subject=*factura* OR All_Email.subject=*recibo* OR All_Email.subject=*comprobante* OR All_Email.subject=*nota fiscal* OR All_Email.subject=*pago* OR All_Email.subject=*SAT*) by All_Email.recipient | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rename recipient as user | stats count as lure_count by user] | where lure_count>0 OR match(cmdline, "(?i)(chr\\(|StrReverse|FromBase64|GetObject\\(\"script|wscript\\.shell)") | table firstTime dest user parent_process process_name cmdline drop_files lure_count
ucs.kql:
let suspectExt = dynamic([".html",".htm",".zip",".msi",".vbs",".vbe",".js"]);
let lureRegex = @"(?i)factura|recibo|comprobante|nota\s?fiscal|SAT|pago|deuda|aviso|fatura|boleto";
let emailDrop = EmailAttachmentInfo
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where FileName has_any (suspectExt)
  | join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where Subject matches regex lureRegex) on NetworkMessageId
  | project Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, SHA256;
let hostChain = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
  | where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe","AutoIt3.exe","AutoIt3_x64.exe")
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("chr(","StrReverse","FromBase64","GetObject(\"script","WScript.Shell")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessParentFileName;
hostChain
| join kind=leftouter (emailDrop | summarize lures=make_set(Subject), senders=make_set(SenderFromAddress) by RecipientEmailAddress)
  on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| extend RegionTag = iff(AccountUpn matches regex @"(?i)\.(mx|br|es|pt|ar|co|cl|pe)$", "LATAM_IBERIA", "OTHER")
| where RegionTag == "LATAM_IBERIA" or array_length(lures) > 0
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Public reporting from SCILabs, Metabase Q, and ESET on Malteiro/Mispadu consistently shows the chain: Spanish/Portuguese-language financial lure -> HTML/ZIP attachment -> MSI executed by user -> msiexec spawning wscript/cscript with heavily obfuscated VBS that decodes an AutoIt loader. Pivoting from msiexec->script-host parent-child (rare in benign software-install telemetry) and correlating with

ucs.title: [LLM] Mispadu credential theft: AutoIt/DLL-sideload process reading browser vaults of LATAM banking targets
ucs.description: Hunts post-execution Mispadu behavior: an AutoIt-compiled or sideloaded binary opening Chromium/Firefox 'Login Data' / logins.json files and querying the system locale to confirm a LATAM/Iberian victim before exfiltrating banking credentials. Combines T1555.003, T1614.001, T1518.001 and T1657 in the exact pattern Malteiro has run since 2019.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as accessed_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path=*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\*\\Login Data* OR Filesystem.file_path=*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data\\*\\Login Data* OR Filesystem.file_path=*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*\\logins.json* OR Filesystem.file_path=*\\Opera Software\\*\\Login Data*) (Filesystem.process_name!=chrome.exe Filesystem.process_name!=msedge.exe Filesystem.process_name!=firefox.exe Filesystem.process_name!=opera.exe) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=AutoIt3.exe OR Processes.process_name=AutoIt3_x64.exe OR Processes.parent_process_name=msiexec.exe OR match(Processes.process, "(?i)(GetUserDefaultUILanguage|chcp 1252|chcp 1250|systeminfo|wmic os get|wmic.*caption)")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process=*ESET* OR Processes.process=*Kaspersky* OR Processes.process=*AVAST* OR Processes.process=*Defender* OR Processes.process=*Bitdefender*) (Processes.process_name=cmd.exe OR Processes.process_name=wmic.exe OR Processes.process_name=tasklist.exe OR Processes.process_name=powershell.exe) by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | stats count as av_recon by dest] | where av_recon>0 OR like(process, "%Language%") | table firstTime dest process_name parent_process_name process accessed_paths av_recon
ucs.kql:
let browserVaults = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",@"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",@"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\",@"\Opera Software\",@"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\"]);
let vaultAccess = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(3d)
  | where (FileName in~ ("Login Data","logins.json","key4.db","key3.db","signons.sqlite","Web Data"))
  | where FolderPath has_any (browserVaults)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, FileName, FolderPath;
let localeOrAvRecon = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(3d)
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("GetUserDefaultUILanguage","chcp 1252","chcp 1250","systeminfo","wmic os get","Get-WinSystemLocale","ESET","Kaspersky","AVAST","Bitdefender","Defender","McAfee","Sophos")
     or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AutoIt3.exe","AutoIt3_x64.exe")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine;
vaultAccess
| join kind=inner (localeOrAvRecon | summarize recon=make_set(ProcessCommandLine) by DeviceName) on DeviceName
| extend Mispadu_Indicators = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AutoIt3.exe","AutoIt3_x64.exe") or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "msiexec.exe", "AutoIt/MSI loader", "Other unsigned binary")
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Mispadu's defining post-exec behavior - documented by SCILabs, ESET, and Metabase Q - is an AutoIt-compiled stealer (often DLL-sideloaded into a benign signed host) that fingerprints the locale (it self-terminates outside LATAM/Iberia), enumerates installed AV (ESET, Kaspersky, etc.), then dumps Chromium 'Login Data' and Firefox 'logins.json' to harvest banking credentials for Mexican/Brazilian/Sp
